title: The colleague who told you the truth
summary: Describe a time when a colleague or classmate gave you honest feedback that was difficult to hear but ultimately useful.
topic: The colleague who told you the truth
prompt: Write 3 paragraphs (350–500 words) describing a time when a colleague, classmate, or teammate gave you honest feedback that was hard to hear but turned out to be helpful. Describe the situation, explain your initial reaction, and reflect on what changed as a result. Use past simple and past perfect, at least one concession with 'although' or 'while', and a modal verb expressing what you should or could have done differently.
hintStarters: The feedback I remember most clearly did not come from a manager or a teacher, but from…
Although I had worked on the project for weeks, my colleague pointed out something I had completely missed…
At the time I heard it, the comment felt unfair, but looking back I can see that…

hintVocab.meaningVi: xem điều gì đó như một sự công kích cá nhân
điểm mù, điều mình không nhận ra
chấp nhận sự khó chịu và suy nghĩ về nó
nhìn lại sau khi sự việc đã xảy ra
tránh làm tổn thương cảm xúc của ai đó
một mối lo ngại thực sự
điều chỉnh cách tiếp cận của mình
bỏ lỡ điểm chính, hiểu sai ý

sampleText:
I had been working at a small translation agency in Warsaw for about six months when a colleague named Marek sat down next to me after a client meeting and said, quietly, that my written tone was coming across as too formal and that clients had mentioned it. I had spent weeks polishing my work and I was proud of it, so my first reaction was to be defensive. I told him that formal language was appropriate for business communication. He nodded, but he had already said what he needed to say and did not push further.

Although I dismissed the comment at the time, I sat with the discomfort for a few days and eventually went back and read my emails from the previous month. Marek was right. I had been writing in a register that would have suited a legal document, not a friendly client update. A phrase like 'please be advised that the aforementioned deliverable has been dispatched' could easily have been 'I've sent the file — let me know if it looks right.' I had been so focused on appearing professional that I had missed the point entirely. What the clients wanted was a genuine concern for their project, not a display of formal vocabulary. I should have asked for feedback earlier instead of assuming that producing clean, correct sentences was enough.

What changed after that conversation was not just my writing style. I started paying attention to how Marek wrote to clients — short, direct, warm — and I adjusted my approach gradually. In hindsight, I am glad he did not hold back. He could have decided to spare my feelings and said nothing, and I would have continued in the same way for another year. Honest feedback, even when it is hard to hear, is a form of respect. It means the other person believes you are capable of doing better. I have tried to carry that lesson into every job I have had since.

gây ấn tượng là quá trang trọng
chấp nhận sự khó chịu và suy nghĩ về nó

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a register that would have suited a legal document
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: văn phong phù hợp với một văn bản pháp lý
hoàn toàn bỏ lỡ điểm chính

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a genuine concern for their project
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: sự quan tâm thực sự đến dự án của họ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: assuming that producing clean, correct sentences was enough
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cho rằng việc tạo ra những câu văn sạch, đúng là đủ
dần dần điều chỉnh cách tiếp cận của mình
tránh làm tổn thương cảm xúc của tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Honest feedback, even when it is hard to hear, is a form of respect
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Phản hồi trung thực, dù khó nghe, là một hình thức tôn trọng

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for earlier background action
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I had been working at the agency for six months' dùng thì quá khứ hoàn thành tiếp diễn để cho thấy một hành động đã diễn ra liên tục trước một thời điểm khác trong quá khứ (cuộc trò chuyện với Marek). Nó nhấn mạnh thời gian và tính liên tục của hành động.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I had been working at the agency for six months' uses the past perfect continuous to show an action that was ongoing before another past point (the conversation with Marek). It emphasises duration and continuity.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Modal 'should have' for past advice
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I should have asked for feedback earlier' dùng 'should have + past participle' để bày tỏ tiếc nuối hoặc lời khuyên về một điều trong quá khứ mà đã không xảy ra. Đây là cấu trúc B1 phổ biến để phản ánh về những sai lầm.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I should have asked for feedback earlier' uses 'should have + past participle' to express regret or retrospective advice about something that did not happen in the past. This is a common B1 structure for reflecting on mistakes.

sampleTranslationVi:
Tôi đã làm việc tại một công ty dịch thuật nhỏ ở Warsaw được khoảng sáu tháng thì một đồng nghiệp tên Marek ngồi xuống cạnh tôi sau một cuộc họp với khách hàng và nói nhỏ rằng văn phong viết của tôi gây ấn tượng là quá trang trọng và các khách hàng đã đề cập đến điều đó. Tôi đã dành nhiều tuần để hoàn thiện công việc của mình và tôi tự hào về nó, vì vậy phản ứng đầu tiên của tôi là có thái độ phòng thủ. Tôi nói với anh ấy rằng ngôn ngữ trang trọng phù hợp với giao tiếp kinh doanh. Anh ấy gật đầu, nhưng anh ấy đã nói những gì cần nói và không thúc ép thêm.

Mặc dù tôi bỏ qua nhận xét đó vào lúc ấy, tôi chấp nhận sự khó chịu và suy nghĩ về nó trong vài ngày, rồi cuối cùng quay lại đọc các email của mình từ tháng trước. Marek đúng. Tôi đã viết theo văn phong phù hợp với một văn bản pháp lý, không phải một bản cập nhật thân thiện cho khách hàng. Một cụm từ như 'xin vui lòng được thông báo rằng tài liệu đã đề cập đã được gửi đi' có thể dễ dàng là 'Tôi đã gửi file — cho tôi biết nếu trông ổn nhé.' Tôi đã quá tập trung vào vẻ chuyên nghiệp đến mức hoàn toàn bỏ lỡ điểm chính. Điều các khách hàng muốn là sự quan tâm thực sự đến dự án của họ, không phải một màn trình diễn từ vựng trang trọng. Lẽ ra tôi nên hỏi xin phản hồi sớm hơn thay vì giả định rằng việc tạo ra những câu văn sạch, đúng là đủ.

Điều thay đổi sau cuộc trò chuyện đó không chỉ là phong cách viết của tôi. Tôi bắt đầu chú ý đến cách Marek viết cho khách hàng — ngắn gọn, thẳng thắn, thân thiện — và dần dần điều chỉnh cách tiếp cận của mình. Nhìn lại, tôi vui vì anh ấy không kìm lại. Anh ấy có thể quyết định tránh làm tổn thương cảm xúc của tôi và không nói gì, và tôi sẽ tiếp tục theo cách đó thêm một năm nữa. Phản hồi trung thực, dù khó nghe, là một hình thức tôn trọng. Điều đó có nghĩa là người kia tin rằng bạn có khả năng làm tốt hơn. Tôi đã cố gắng mang theo bài học đó vào mọi công việc kể từ đó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses past perfect continuous for background duration?
options: I had been working at the agency for six months when Marek spoke to me.
I was working at the agency for six months when Marek spoke to me.
I had worked at the agency since six months when Marek spoke to me.
I have been working at the agency for six months when Marek spoke to me.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect continuous ('had been working') shows an ongoing action before a past event. 'For' is used with a duration; the main event uses past simple.
mcQuestions.hint: Past perfect continuous = 'had been + -ing'. Use 'for' with a period of time.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses 'should have' correctly to express past regret?
options: I should have asked for feedback earlier instead of assuming I was right.
I should asked for feedback earlier instead of assuming I was right.
I should have ask for feedback earlier instead of assuming I was right.
I should to have asked for feedback earlier instead of assuming I was right.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Should have + past participle' expresses past regret. The structure is 'should have asked', not 'should asked' or 'should have ask'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Should have' is always followed by the past participle.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'although' correctly to introduce a concession?
options: Although I dismissed the comment at the time, I sat with the discomfort for a few days.
Although I dismissed the comment at the time, but I sat with the discomfort for a few days.
Although, I dismissed the comment at the time, I sat with the discomfort for a few days.
I dismissed the comment at the time although, I sat with the discomfort for a few days.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although + clause, main clause' — no comma after 'although' and no 'but' before the main clause.
mcQuestions.hint: No comma after 'although'; no 'but' in the main clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly uses 'could have' to describe an unchosen past action?
options: He could have decided to spare my feelings and said nothing.
He could have decided to spare my feelings and say nothing.
He could decided to spare my feelings and said nothing.
He could to have decided to spare my feelings and said nothing.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Could have + past participle' describes a past possibility that was not chosen. 'Said' is parallel to 'decided' — both past participles after 'could have'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Could have' + past participle. Keep parallel structure when listing two actions.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase uses 'coming across as' correctly?
options: My written tone was coming across as too formal.
My written tone was coming across too formal.
My written tone was coming across like too formal.
My written tone came across as too formally.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Come across as + adjective' is the correct structure. 'Too formally' would need an adjective, not an adverb, after 'as'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Come across as' is followed by an adjective, not an adverb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'instead of' to contrast two actions?
options: I should have asked for feedback instead of assuming everything was fine.
I should have asked for feedback instead assume everything was fine.
I should have asked for feedback instead of to assume everything was fine.
I should have asked for feedback instead assumed everything was fine.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Instead of' is a preposition and must be followed by a gerund ('assuming'), not a bare infinitive or past tense.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses 'gradually' in the correct position?
options: I adjusted my approach gradually over the following months.
I adjusted gradually my approach over the following months.
Gradually I was adjusting my approach over the following months.
I was adjusted my approach gradually over the following months.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverbs of manner like 'gradually' typically follow the verb and object. 'I adjusted my approach gradually' is natural and correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Adverbs of manner usually go after the verb and its object.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'in hindsight' as a discourse marker?
options: In hindsight, I am glad he did not hold back.
In hindsight I am glad he did not hold back.
In hindsight I am glad, he did not hold back.
In hindsight; I am glad he did not hold back.
mcQuestions.explanation: Discourse markers at the start of a sentence are followed by a comma: 'In hindsight, I am glad…'
mcQuestions.hint: Introductory discourse markers are followed by a comma.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which relative clause is formed correctly?
options: I started paying attention to how Marek wrote to clients — short, direct, warm.
I started paying attention to how Marek was writing to clients — short, direct, warm.
I started paying attention to how Marek writes to clients — short, direct, warm.
I started to pay attention how Marek wrote to clients — short, direct, warm.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Paying attention to how + clause' is correct. Past simple 'wrote' fits the narrative context. 'Pay attention to how' requires 'to' before the indirect question.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Pay attention to how + clause' — don't drop the 'to'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'mean + that' to explain significance?
options: It means the other person believes you are capable of doing better.
It means that the other person believe you are capable of doing better.
It means the other person is believe you are capable to do better.
It means that the other person believes you are capable of do better.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'It means (that) + full clause' — the verb in the clause ('believes') agrees with its subject; 'capable of + gerund' ('doing').
mcQuestions.hint: 'Capable of' is followed by a gerund (-ing), not a bare infinitive.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly uses 'would have continued' to describe an avoided outcome?
options: I would have continued in the same way for another year.
I would continued in the same way for another year.
I would have continue in the same way for another year.
I had continued in the same way for another year.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Would have + past participle' describes a hypothetical past outcome that did not happen because of an intervening action.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Would have' + past participle for third conditional or hypothetical past outcomes.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is there a difference between honest feedback and unkind feedback? What makes difficult truth-telling feel respectful rather than hurtful, and does the relationship between two people change what is appropriate to say?
